rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant San Francisco Gravel Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Mr. Tremblay, Mr. Nicolai, Mr. Robinson and Mr. Chaney and the declaration of Mr. Templin declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Tremblay was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing and joint compound) attributable to defendant. As to defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication, it is denied in its entirety for the same reasons stated above. The only ground upon which defendant moved for each summary adjudication issue, as stated in its Notice of Motion and repeated in its Separate Statement, was identical to the ground upon which it moved for summary judgment. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Robinson and Mr. Chaney are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Templin's declaration are sustained only in that defendant objected that Mr. Templin did not attach any documents to his declaration. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' references in their separate statement to portions of Mr. Tremblay's deposition testimony that plaintiffs failed to attach are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
